content.problemStatement:
Vlad enjoys listening to music. He lives in Sam's Town. A few days ago he had a birthday, so his parents gave him a gift: MP3-player! Vlad was the happiest man in the world! Now he can listen his favorite songs whenever he wants!

Vlad built up his own playlist. The playlist consists of **N** songs, each has a **unique** positive integer length. Vlad likes all the songs from his playlist, but there is a song, which he likes more than the others. It's named "Uncle Johny".

After creation of the playlist, Vlad decided to sort the songs in increasing order of their lengths. For example, if the lengths of the songs in playlist was {1, 3, 5, 2, 4} after sorting it becomes {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}. Before the sorting, "Uncle Johny" was on **K**-th position (1-indexing is assumed for the playlist) in the playlist.

Vlad needs your help! He gives you all the information of his playlist. Your task is to find the position of "Uncle Johny" in  the sorted playlist.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K, where N is the number of songs and K is the position of 'Uncle Johny' in the unsorted playlist. The second line contains N integers representing the lengths of the songs.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the position of 'Uncle Johny' in the sorted playlist.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: In the first test case, the unsorted array is [1, 3, 4, 2]. After sorting, it becomes [1, 2, 3, 4]. The position of 'Uncle Johny' (which is the second element in the unsorted array) is 3 in the sorted array.

Explanation: In this test case, the unsorted array is [1, 2, 3, 9, 4]. After sorting, it becomes [1, 2, 3, 4, 9]. The position of 'Uncle Johny' (which is the fifth element in the unsorted array) is 4 in the sorted array.

Explanation: In this test case, the unsorted array is [1, 2, 3, 9, 4]. After sorting, it becomes [1, 2, 3, 4, 9]. The position of 'Uncle Johny' (which is the first element in the unsorted array) is 1 in the sorted array.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by sorting the array of song lengths and then finding the position of 'Uncle Johny' in the sorted array. The key insight is to first find the length of 'Uncle Johny' in the unsorted array and then find its position in the sorted array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total. The sorting operation takes O(N log N) time and it is performed for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the array of song lengths.

code.Python3:
import sys
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N, K = map(int, input().split())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    target = arr[K - 1]
    arr.sort()
    pos = arr.index(target) + 1
    print(pos)
